$comment: Handoff briefing for the next Claude Code session on this repo. Open the desktop/CLI Claude Code app on Paperfly, paste this whole file as the first message, then proceed.

session_meta.remote_state: pushed and up to date as of handoff

what_was_just_shipped.summary: Three back-to-back gameplay layers landed in this branch. The game now has player progression (combo + skins), tactical choice (power-ups), visual variety (env phases), and harder reads (drift/splitter obstacles). Tuning has NOT been validated on-device yet.
commits_in_order: e4e844d feat: 4 new skins, combo multiplier, 3 new achievements
3672e92 feat(game): dynamic sky environments shift with score
d31283c feat(game): drifting and splitting obstacles

subsystems.skins: 11 total (6 free, 5 rare). Unlock conditions in frontend/src/game/skins.ts span levels 3-12 + combo/ring/score/survival achievements.
subsystems.combo: Chained coin collection. ×1 -> ×5 at a 12-coin streak. 4-second lapse window. Refs: comboRef, comboExpiresRef in frontend/app/game.tsx ~line 139.
subsystems.powerups: shield / magnet / slowmo. ~5 % of spawned objects. Refs: shieldRef, magnetUntilRef, slowmoUntilRef. Effective dt is used everywhere, so slow-mo correctly scales drift + spawn pacing.
subsystems.environments: Dawn -> Day -> Sunset -> Night -> Storm. Table in frontend/src/game/environment.ts. Game loop pushes to React state only on bucket change. LinearGradient.colors and ParallaxClouds.cloudColor both follow env.
subsystems.obstacles: 25 % drift (lateral vx 35-70 units/s, bounce at PLANE_X_RANGE * 1.3), 10 % split (spawns 2 children at z<250 with vx +/-70, child size = parent * 0.62). Both behaviors stack.

next_session_goal.theme: Polish & juice. No new systems. Make the existing ones FEEL better. Goal: a 60-second run should have at least 3-4 moments that make the player smile or grip the phone tighter.
non_goals: Do NOT add daily challenges, missions, leaderboards, or new obstacle types.
Do NOT rebalance difficulty curves yet (speedRef ramp, spawn density). Tuning constants only when explicitly listed below.
Do NOT refactor projection.ts or the useGameLoop pattern. They are load-bearing.
Do NOT add backend/Supabase calls. This is a client-side polish pass.
next_session_goal.definition_of_done: Each polish item below shipped as its own commit on the same branch, pushed, and verified by starting the dev server (npx expo start) and confirming it on a real phone via Expo Go. Type check passes (cd frontend && yarn tsc --noEmit).

first_tasks_in_order.title: Tune power-up spawn rate down
first_tasks_in_order.why: 5 % feels too generous in playtesting notes. Power-ups should feel rare-but-earned, not constant.
first_tasks_in_order.change: Drop to 0.03 (3 %). Also adjust the ring/obstacle split: `else if (r0 < 0.60)` instead of 0.62, to keep obstacles at ~37 %.
first_tasks_in_order.validation: Play a 60s run. You should see ~1-2 power-ups, not 4-5.

first_tasks_in_order.title: Make splitter visual unmissable
first_tasks_in_order.why: Yellow border (3px) does not read clearly in motion. Player gets hit by a child obstacle and feels cheated.
first_tasks_in_order.line_hint: ~line 931, the splitter borderColor branch in the obstacle render.
change_options: Pulse the border opacity using a sine of `now * 0.008` so it visibly throbs.
Render a small inner crosshair/X line on top (2 thin Views forming an X) for splitters.
Tint the fill itself slightly desaturated + add a thin glow View behind via shadowColor + elevation (already in styles).
first_tasks_in_order.recommended: Combine #1 (pulsing border) with a brighter fill hue when willSplit && !hasSplit. Keep it cheap — no animations API, just compute in render from `now`.
first_tasks_in_order.validation: On a phone, splitters should be obvious from spawn distance. A friend watching over your shoulder should be able to point them out.

first_tasks_in_order.title: Combo tier-up moment
first_tasks_in_order.why: Hitting ×3, ×4, ×5 currently just updates a number. Should feel like a milestone.
first_tasks_in_order.line_hint: Search for `comboRef.current += 1` (~line 700-720). The popup is queued there.
first_tasks_in_order.change: When the combo tier increases (combo crosses 3, 7, 12 -> the breakpoints used for the ×2/×3/×4/×5 multiplier in the existing scoring formula — verify in code), push an extra popup with label like 'COMBO ×2!', and trigger a one-shot haptic via expo-haptics (already in package.json — confirm). Also briefly scale the score HUD via a transient state.
subtasks: Add a comboTierRef to track the last announced tier so we don't re-fire on every coin.
On tier increase: haptic Notification(Success), queue tier popup, set scoreFlashUntilRef = now + 350.
In the HUD render, read scoreFlashUntilRef via state (or a ref-driven re-render trick) and apply a transform: scale(1.15) for the flash window.
first_tasks_in_order.validation: Picking up the 3rd coin in a streak should produce a clearly audible/tactile tier-up. Same at 7 and 12.

first_tasks_in_order.title: Environment phase transition flash
first_tasks_in_order.why: Crossing into Sunset/Night currently shows only a text popup. The sky change itself is abrupt.
first_tasks_in_order.change: On phase change, set a `phaseFlashUntilRef = now + 600`. Render a full-screen white View on top with opacity that fades from 0.45 -> 0 over the 600ms window. Also haptic Notification(Warning) to mark the shift.
first_tasks_in_order.extra_polish: The label popup ('NIGHT SKY') is currently the same size as score popups. Bump it up: bigger font, center it, fade in/out instead of float up. Consider a dedicated popup `kind` field on PopupItem.
first_tasks_in_order.validation: Hitting 500 / 1500 / 3000 / 6000 score should feel like an act break, not just a UI tick.

first_tasks_in_order.title: Ring collect burst
first_tasks_in_order.why: Coins disappear silently. Each grab is the core loop reward — it should pop.
first_tasks_in_order.change: On coin collect, spawn 5-7 short-lived particle Views (lifetime 250ms) that radiate outward from the collect point with vx/vy randomized. Store in a particlesRef array updated in the same game loop tick. Render as plain absolutely-positioned Views — no SVG, no Reanimated.
first_tasks_in_order.scope_guard: Keep particles dumb: position, lifetime, color. No physics. Reuse the same struct shape as popupsRef.
first_tasks_in_order.validation: Coin grab should feel like a small firework. Combo tier-ups should make the particle count bigger (multiply by combo tier).

first_tasks_in_order.title: Near-miss bonus
first_tasks_in_order.why: Threading between two close obstacles is the most exciting moment a runner has. Currently nothing rewards it.
first_tasks_in_order.line_hint: In the per-frame loop, the obstacle collision block (~line 630).
first_tasks_in_order.change: When an obstacle passes the plane's Z plane (just behind, say z dropped below 30 last frame) AND the screen-space distance between plane and obstacle center was < 90 px at min, award +25, queue a 'NEAR MISS +25' popup, haptic light impact.
first_tasks_in_order.scope_guard: Track per-obstacle `closestDist` and `passed` flags. Don't fire more than once per obstacle. Don't fire while shield is active (the shield path uses different geometry).
first_tasks_in_order.validation: Squeezing past a wall on either side should give a tiny rush + score bump.

conventions_to_respect.state_vs_refs: Anything that mutates every frame lives in a useRef (objectsRef, popupsRef, speedRef, comboRef, *UntilRef). React state is reserved for things that should trigger a re-render (HUD numbers, env, shieldActive). NEVER setState every frame.
conventions_to_respect.effective_dt: All time-based math must multiply by `effectiveDt`, not raw dt. Slow-mo scales effectiveDt by 0.45. Drift, spawn timer, env progression, particles all need this.
conventions_to_respect.projection: Screen position is `cxNow + (o.x - planeWorldX * 0.4) * scale`, `scale = FOCAL / o.z`. Constants are in frontend/src/game/projection.ts. Do not duplicate this math elsewhere — import from projection.ts.
conventions_to_respect.spawn_pipeline: All world objects go through the single spawn block ~line 540 of game.tsx. New behaviors should be flags on WorldObj (see willSplit/hasSplit/vx in projection.ts), not new top-level systems.
conventions_to_respect.performance_budget: Target 60 fps on a mid-tier Android. Don't add Reanimated, Skia, or SVG. Plain Views + transforms only. Render functions for objects are mapped from objectsRef each frame — keep per-object work cheap.
conventions_to_respect.no_new_files_unless_needed: Prefer extending existing modules (Hud.tsx, environment.ts, projection.ts). Only create a new file if the concept is genuinely separable (Particles.tsx is a reasonable candidate).

files_to_read_first.why: Main game loop, spawn, collision, render. Most polish work happens here.
WorldObj type + projection constants. New fields go here.
HUD render. Combo tier flash and phase-transition overlay live near this.
Env table. Reference, do not edit without reason.
Skins + unlock conditions. Read only — do not add new ones.

verification_steps_per_commit: cd frontend && yarn tsc --noEmit  -- must pass
npx expo start  -- open in Expo Go on a real phone
Play one 60-90s run. Confirm the polish item is visible and feels good.
git add -A && git commit -m "<type>(game): <change>" && git push

git_rules.commit_style: Conventional commits with a body explaining WHY. Look at recent commits in this branch for tone — they explain reasoning, not just changes.
git_rules.no_pr_yet: Do not open a PR unless the user explicitly asks. Push to the branch only.
git_rules.no_amend: Always create new commits, never amend. Each polish task = its own commit.

known_pitfalls: comboExpiresRef is set to `now + 4000` in ms but `now` comes from performance.now — keep units consistent.
popupsRef cleanup runs every frame (filter > 1s). New popup kinds with longer lifetimes need their own cleanup threshold or a `ttl` field.
Splitter spawns its 2 children at z = o.z + 4 to avoid an immediate re-trigger of any z<X check on the same frame. Preserve this offset.
The dailyRngRef is used for daily challenges; when present, all spawn rolls must go through it. Don't bypass it with Math.random in new code.
seed_questions_to_answer_at_session_star: Did you start the dev server and load on a real phone? Polish work cannot be validated in a simulator.
After tuning power-ups to 3 %, did a 60s run still surface at least one power-up?
Does the splitter now read clearly from spawn distance, or does it still need iteration?
Do the combo tier-up haptics fire on Android AND iOS? (expo-haptics support differs)
stretch_if_time_remains: Subtle CRT/film grain over the night and storm environments only.
Tilt the plane sprite slightly more during slow-mo for a 'time bends' look.
Trail effect behind the plane that intensifies with combo tier.
